rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 9 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) as the unsafe copy, explained the command-line argument can exceed the 256-byte buffer, and explicitly called it a stack-buffer-overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They used strncpy with explicit NUL termination but did not actually reject overlong input before copying (only mentioned it as an optional idea), which the criterion requires.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended eliminating unbounded string handling by dynamically sizing the buffer or validating/rejecting argv length up front, which satisfies the root-cause/global fix requirement.
